best_for: Marketing sites needing Google Ads integration, free tier at scale, and Search Console linkage
avoid_when: Product analytics (funnels by user), teams who need raw event access without BigQuery, strict-privacy positioning
tradeoffs: Free and ubiquitous; sampled/modeled data, consent-mode dependency, and a UI everyone struggles with

best_for: Privacy-first marketing sites: one script, no cookies, EU hosting, public dashboards
avoid_when: Product analytics, user-level funnels/retention — it's aggregate by design
tradeoffs: Cookieless simplicity often removes the consent-banner need for analytics; paid, and deliberately shallow per-user data

best_for: Plausible-style privacy analytics at zero license cost on your own infra
tradeoffs: Free and self-owned; you run the database, updates, and scaling yourself

best_for: SaaS product analytics: user-level funnels, retention, session replay, feature flags, A/B tests in one tool — generous free tier, EU cloud option
avoid_when: A pure brochure site (overkill); teams that only need pageview counts
tradeoffs: One tool replaces 3-4 point solutions; event volume pricing needs watching once traffic grows

best_for: Mature product orgs standardizing on dedicated behavioral analytics with strong cohort/journey tooling
avoid_when: Early-stage products (PostHog covers it cheaper); marketing-only measurement
tradeoffs: Best-in-class analysis UIs; costs climb steeply with event volume and seats

best_for: Data teams that want raw, unsampled events under their own SQL and BI stack
avoid_when: No data team — a warehouse without an owner is a swamp
tradeoffs: Full control and joinability with business data; you build the dashboards and the pipeline yourself

best_for: Ad-blocker resilience, first-party data control, trimming what reaches vendors — pairs with the Ads module's server-side tracking checks
avoid_when: Simple sites where the added infra outweighs signal loss
tradeoffs: Recovers 10-30% of events lost to blockers; another service to run and keep consent-compliant

option: Web + product analytics as two tools
best_for: Marketing site on Plausible/GA4 + app on PostHog: each tool does what it's actually good at
avoid_when: Tiny teams where one tool must serve both (pick PostHog with pageview autocapture)
tradeoffs: Clean separation of concerns; two configs, two consent entries, and cross-domain journey stitching to think about

best_for: Debugging UX friction, support escalations, watching real onboarding runs
avoid_when: Recording without masking — replay captures everything users type
tradeoffs: High-empathy debugging; strict PII masking and consent coverage are non-negotiable before enabling

option: Feature flags + A/B testing tied to analytics
best_for: Shipping experiments where exposure and outcome live in the same event stream (PostHog/GrowthBook)
avoid_when: Peeking at results daily and stopping at the first significant blip
tradeoffs: Same-tool experiments avoid identity-joining pain; statistical discipline still on you (fixed horizon or sequential method)

best_for: Knowing which channel drives signups: persist first/last-touch UTMs on the user record at signup
avoid_when: Trusting any single ad platform's self-reported attribution as ground truth
tradeoffs: Cheap and platform-neutral; undercounts dark social/direct — treat as directional, cross-check with MER (see data/ads/tracking-attribution-checks.csv)

best_for: EU/KVKK traffic: load analytics only after consent, or choose a cookieless tool that documents its no-consent basis
avoid_when: Assuming a cookie banner alone makes GA4 compliant — configuration does
tradeoffs: Legal safety and cleaner data contracts; consent refusals create a measurement blind spot to model
